reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All six gates passed. Gate 3 (Code Verification): Confirmed that put_rpc_custody_columns (L240-267) performs KZG verification but never updates observed_column_sidecars cache, while the gossip path calls observe_gossip_data_column (L521 data_column_verification.rs) which does update the cache. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this via P2P DataColumnsByRoot RPC by first publishing column C1 via gossip (gets cached), then serving conflicting column C2 (same slot/proposer/index, different data) via RPC to specific peers, causing network inconsistency. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 299 requires 'Anti-equivocation cache must be updated for all imported data columns regardless of publishing.' The RPC path violates this by only updating observed_slashable (which allows multiple block_roots) instead of observed_column_sidecars (which enforces single data per index). Severity remains High: allows network split across nodes that received different data for the same column index, affecting data availability sampling integrity for >33% of network if exploited systematically.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Anti-equivocation cache must be updated for all imported data columns regardless of publishing. Assertion: forall col: imported(col) => anti_equivocation_cache.contains(col.key)
